notifications.text: Clear skies forecast for sunrise at Angkor Wat on Day 1 — perfect conditions.
Sok added Phare Cambodian Circus to Day 3 — sells out fast, tickets pre-booked.
Angkor pass prices verified: 1-day $37, 3-day $62, 7-day $72. You have a 1-day pass.
Cambodia uses USD alongside Khmer Riel (KHR). $1 ≈ 4,100 KHR. Small change often given in riel.
All 7 days synced for offline access. Maps and directions cached.

changeLog.title: Angkor Wat Sunrise
changeLog.reason: Earlier pickup to beat the crowds at east gate

changeLog.title: Bamboo Train
changeLog.reason: Cooler in the morning, less crowded

items.title: Angkor Wat Sunrise
items.detail: Arrive early for the reflection pool. Enter via east gate. Angkor pass required ($37/day). Bring a flashlight for the dark corridors.
items.directions: 15 min tuk-tuk from Siem Reap center. Driver picks up at hotel.

items.title: Breakfast at Hotel
items.detail: Included buffet at Shinta Mani. Khmer noodle soup station, fresh tropical fruit, strong Cambodian coffee.

items.title: Angkor Thom & Bayon Temple
items.detail: Enter through the South Gate — lined with 54 stone giants. Bayon has 216 serene stone faces. Allow 2 hours. Sok will explain the bas-reliefs.
items.directions: 10 min drive north from Angkor Wat. Stay on the main temple circuit road.

items.title: Ta Prohm (Tomb Raider Temple)
items.detail: Giant silk-cotton trees swallowing stone walls. The most photogenic temple. Visit the 'Angelina Jolie tree' — the famous doorway from the film.
items.directions: 15 min from Bayon, east on the inner circuit road.

items.title: Lunch at Mahob Khmer
items.detail: Traditional Khmer cuisine in a wooden house. Try the fish amok ($6) and morning glory stir-fry ($4). Reserve the garden table.
items.directions: Wat Bo area, 5 min tuk-tuk from the temple zone.

items.title: Banteay Kdei & Srah Srang
items.detail: Quieter temple with beautiful carvings. Then walk to Srah Srang — the royal bathing pool. Good spot to rest and photograph the water.

items.title: Sunset at Phnom Bakheng
items.detail: Hilltop temple with panoramic sunset views over Angkor Wat. Limited to 300 visitors — arrive by 4:30 PM. Steep climb, about 15 min.
items.directions: Between Angkor Wat and Angkor Thom. 400m uphill walk from the road.

items.title: Pub Street & Night Market
items.detail: Lively strip in old town. $0.50 draft beers, street-side fish BBQ, and insect carts if you dare. Pick up souvenirs at the night market next door.
items.directions: Heart of Siem Reap. Walk from most central hotels.

items.title: Breakfast at Blue Pumpkin
items.detail: Popular bakery-cafe on Pub Street. Excellent pastries, iced coffee, and eggs benedict ($5). Upstairs bean-bag lounge is iconic.

items.title: Banteay Srei
items.detail: The 'Citadel of Women' — intricate pink sandstone carvings, the finest in all of Angkor. 45 min drive but absolutely worth it. Allow 1.5 hours.
items.directions: 37 km northeast of Siem Reap. Paved road the whole way.

items.title: Kbal Spean (River of a Thousand Lingas)
items.detail: Sacred riverbed carved with Hindu symbols. 1.5 km jungle hike each way — moderate difficulty. Bring water and sturdy shoes. Waterfall at the end.
items.directions: 15 km past Banteay Srei, then a 30 min hike uphill.

items.title: Lunch at a Roadside Khmer BBQ
items.detail: Stop at a local roadside grill on the way back. Grilled chicken, sticky rice, and green mango salad — about $3–4 per person. Pure Cambodian countryside eating.

items.title: Kompong Khleang Floating Village
items.detail: Stilted village on Tonle Sap — more authentic than Kompong Phluk. Boat tour through the village ($15/person). Houses on 10m stilts, floating school. Respectful visit with local guide.
items.directions: 50 km east of Siem Reap. Mr. Vann drives, then boat from the dock.

items.title: Khmer Massage at Frangipani Spa
items.detail: Traditional Khmer massage ($12/hour). Hot stone option available. Perfect after two days of temple walking. Book the couples room.
items.directions: Sivatha Blvd, central Siem Reap. 5 min walk from Pub Street.

items.title: Dinner at Cuisine Wat Damnak
items.detail: Michelin-recognized Khmer tasting menu ($28/person). Six courses using local ingredients — smoked fish, Kampot pepper, palm sugar. Reservations essential.
items.directions: Wat Damnak area, east side of the river. Tuk-tuk $2 from center.

items.title: Drive to Battambang
items.detail: 3-hour scenic drive via NH6. Rolling rice paddies and sugar palm trees the whole way. Stop at Sisophon for fuel and snacks.
items.directions: NH6 west from Siem Reap, then NH5 south to Battambang.

items.title: Bamboo Train (Norry)
items.detail: Rickety bamboo platform on old French railway tracks — a uniquely Cambodian experience. $5/person for the ride. One track, so one train must disassemble when they meet!
items.directions: 7 km south of Battambang center, near O Dambong village.

items.title: Battambang Colonial Architecture Walk
items.detail: French colonial buildings along the Sangker River. See the old governor's residence, the central market, and beautiful shophouse facades. Battambang has the best-preserved colonial architecture in Cambodia.

items.title: Lunch at Jaan Bai
items.detail: Social enterprise restaurant training former street youth. Excellent Khmer fusion — try the Battambang orange curry ($5) and rice-flour crepes. Beautiful garden courtyard.

items.title: Phnom Sampeau & Bat Caves
items.detail: Hilltop temple with dark Khmer Rouge history (killing caves memorial). At 5:30 PM, millions of bats stream out of the cave — an unforgettable natural spectacle. Allow 3 hours total.
items.directions: 12 km southwest of Battambang. Mr. Vann drives, then climb 700 steps.

items.title: Phare Cambodian Circus
items.detail: World-class circus by former street children. Acrobatics, music, and storytelling that tells Cambodian history. $18/person, $28 for VIP with drinks. Truly moving — the highlight of Battambang.
items.directions: Phare Ponleu Selpak campus, west of the river.

items.title: Dinner at Smokin' Pot
items.detail: Riverside restaurant with great atmosphere. Khmer curry in clay pots ($5), cold Angkor beer ($1.50). Sit on the terrace overlooking the Sangker River.

items.title: Drive to Phnom Penh
items.detail: 5-hour drive via NH5 through the Cambodian heartland. Flat roads, rice fields, and small market towns. Stop in Pursat for coconut water.
items.directions: NH5 south from Battambang through Pursat to Phnom Penh.

items.title: Lunch at Friends Restaurant
items.detail: Another social enterprise training at-risk youth. Excellent Khmer tapas ($3–5 each). The spring rolls and lok lak are standout. Rooftop terrace with city views.
items.directions: Street 13, near the National Museum.

items.title: Royal Palace & Silver Pagoda
items.detail: Stunning Khmer architecture — the Throne Hall is breathtaking. Silver Pagoda floor has 5,000+ silver tiles. $10 entry. Dress code: cover knees and shoulders. Allow 2 hours.
items.directions: Riverside, central Phnom Penh. Walk south from the National Museum.

items.title: Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum (S-21)
items.detail: Former high school turned Khmer Rouge prison. Haunting and essential. Audio guide ($3) is highly recommended. Emotionally difficult but important for understanding Cambodia. Allow 1.5–2 hours.
items.directions: Street 113, Boeung Keng Kang. 15 min tuk-tuk from the palace.

items.title: Sisowath Quay Riverside Walk
items.detail: Promenade along the Tonle Sap and Mekong confluence. Locals exercising, monks in orange robes, street vendors selling grilled corn. Golden hour light is magical here.
items.directions: Waterfront road, stretches 3 km north from the Royal Palace.

items.title: Dinner at Romdeng
items.detail: Fine Khmer dining in a colonial villa with pool. Famous for its tarantula fritters (yes, really) and tree-ant beef dip. Regular dishes are superb too — try the Mekong fish in banana leaf ($8).
items.directions: Street 174, 10 min walk from the riverside.

items.title: Check in at Plantation Urban Resort
items.detail: Boutique hotel in a restored 1930s mansion. Lush garden, saltwater pool, quiet neighborhood. Rooms from $85/night. Walking distance to riverside.

items.title: Drive to Kampot
items.detail: 3-hour drive south on NH3. Flat lowlands give way to dramatic karst mountains as you approach Kampot. Beautiful final stretch along the river.
items.directions: NH3 south from Phnom Penh. Well-paved highway the whole way.

items.title: La Plantation Pepper Farm
items.detail: World-famous Kampot pepper — tour the farm, learn about black, white, and red pepper varieties. Free tasting. Gift shop has excellent prices ($8/bag). The countryside drive alone is gorgeous.
items.directions: 18 km north of Kampot town, near Phnom Voar mountain.

items.title: Lunch at Epic Arts Cafe
items.detail: Social enterprise employing deaf and disabled Cambodians. Amazing shakes, fresh salads, and Khmer dishes ($3–5). The crab fried rice with Kampot pepper is incredible.
items.directions: Old Market area, central Kampot.

items.title: Bokor Hill Station
items.detail: Abandoned French hill station at 1,080m. Eerie old casino, church ruins, and Black Palace. Sweeping views of the coast and jungle. Much cooler temperature up top. $2 park entry.
items.directions: 42 km winding mountain road from Kampot. Mr. Vann drives (1 hour up).

items.title: Kampot River Sunset Cruise
items.detail: Rent a paddleboard or kayak ($5/hour) on the Praek Tuek Chhu River. Or just sit at a riverside bar. The sunset behind the mountains is one of Cambodia's most beautiful scenes.
items.directions: Riverside road, south of the old bridge. Rentals near the fish market.

items.title: Dinner at The Fish Market
items.detail: Open-air riverside restaurant — catch of the day grilled whole ($7–10). Kampot pepper crab is legendary ($12). Cold draught beer, fairy lights, river breeze. This is peak Cambodia.
items.directions: On the river, south end of town. Impossible to miss.

items.title: Check in at Rikitikitavi
items.detail: Quirky boutique hotel above a popular restaurant. Rooftop pool with river views. Rooms from $55/night. Great cocktails at the bar. Central location.

items.title: Drive to Kep
items.detail: Quick 30-min drive along the coast. Pass through salt flats and crab farms. Kep was Cambodia's glamorous beach resort in the 1960s — abandoned villas still dot the hillside.
items.directions: NH33A east from Kampot. Coastal road with sea views.

items.title: Kep Crab Market
items.detail: Famous open-air market right on the water. Choose your live crab, they grill it with Kampot pepper sauce ($5–8 per crab). Best seafood experience in Cambodia. Eat at the stilted platforms over the sea.
items.directions: Kep Beach Road, south end. Look for the blue crab statue.

items.title: Rabbit Island (Koh Tonsay)
items.detail: 20-min boat ride ($8 return) to a sleepy island with palm-fringed beaches. Hammocks, basic bungalows, zero development. Swim, nap, repeat. Bring snacks — limited food options.
items.directions: Boats depart from Kep pier, near the crab market. Last boat back at 4 PM.

items.title: Lunch on Rabbit Island
items.detail: Simple grilled fish and rice at a beach shack ($4). Cold drinks in a bucket of ice. Toes in the sand. This is the Cambodia that the package tourists never see.

items.title: Boat Back to Kep
items.detail: Last comfortable boat back. Catch it by 3:30 PM to have time for the national park hike.

items.title: Kep National Park Hike
items.detail: 8 km loop trail through coastal jungle. Led Zep trail has the best viewpoints — panoramic views of the Gulf of Thailand and Vietnam in the distance. $1 entry. Bring water.
items.directions: Trailhead opposite the main Kep Beach.

items.title: Sunset Dinner at Sailing Club
items.detail: Breezy terrace facing the sea. Grilled squid, prawn skewers, and Kampot pepper everything. Cocktails from $4. Watch the sunset over Rabbit Island. A perfect ending to the coast.
items.directions: Kep Beach Road, near the white horse statue.

items.title: Early Drive to Sihanoukville
items.detail: 3.5-hour drive to the coast. Highway is good. Arrive in time for the morning ferry. Mr. Vann will wait with luggage for the return trip.
items.directions: NH3 and NH4 via Kampot to Sihanoukville port.

items.title: Speed Ferry to Koh Rong
items.detail: 45-min speed ferry ($12 one-way). Can be bumpy — take motion sickness meds if needed. Arrive at Koh Toch pier. Crystal clear water visible from the boat.
items.directions: Serendipity Pier or Ochheuteal Pier, Sihanoukville.

items.title: Long Set Beach (7km Beach)
items.detail: One of Southeast Asia's most beautiful beaches — 7 km of powder-white sand with almost nobody on it. Wade in turquoise shallows. The water is impossibly clear.
items.directions: 20-min walk or $5 boat taxi from Koh Toch pier.

items.title: Beach Lunch at Treehouse Bungalows
items.detail: Wood-fired pizza and fresh seafood right on the sand. Mango smoothie ($2), grilled prawn plate ($6). Hammocks and swings between palm trees.

items.title: Snorkeling at Coral Garden
items.detail: Boat trip to coral reef off the west coast ($15/person including gear). Sea horses, clownfish, blue starfish. Visibility 10-15m in dry season. Non-swimmers can use life vests.
items.directions: Arranged through Koh Toch dive shops. 20 min boat ride.

items.title: Last Swim & Bioluminescence Briefing
items.detail: Final swim at the beach. If you ever return, Koh Rong has the world's best bioluminescent plankton (visible Oct–May at night). For now, soak up the last afternoon.

items.title: Ferry Back to Sihanoukville
items.detail: Last speed ferry of the day ($12). Mr. Vann meets at the pier for the drive to Phnom Penh airport (3.5 hours) or onward travel.
items.directions: Koh Toch pier. Be there 15 min early.

items.title: Arrive Phnom Penh International Airport
items.detail: Mr. Vann drops at departures. Allow 2 hours for check-in. Duty-free has Kampot pepper and Angkor cookies — last chance for gifts. Som arkoun, Cambodia! (Thank you!)
items.directions: Phnom Penh International Airport (PNH). 30 min from city center.
